title: Oldenlandia lapeyrousei
taxonomy: Oldenlandia lapeyrousei belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as madder or coffee family. The genus Oldenlandia is a part of this family and consists of annual or perennial herbs found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Oldenlandia lapeyrousei is further supported by its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies. Its classification is also closely related to other genera within the Rubiaceae family, such as Oldenlandia and Paederota.
uses: Oldenlandia lapeyrousei has been used in traditional medicine, although its effectiveness and safety are not well-documented. It is often used in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Oldenlandia lapeyrousei for medicinal purposes. Additionally, its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.

preparations: Oldenlandia lapeyrousei is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Pacific Islands and Australia, where it has been employed for various health purposes. Its herbal preparations are typically made from the plant's leaves, which are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. side_effects: Oldenlandia lapeyrousei is reported to have potential side effects, which may include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal issues. intro: Oldenlandia lapeyrousei, also known as Lapeyrous' oldenlandia or Lapeyrous' creeping buttonweed, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia lapeyrousei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oldenlandia lapeyrousei is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, to take advantage of its health benefits.
